info.title: Panopto Public API
info.description: The public API for Panopto. OpenID & OAuth information can be found at Panopto/oauth2/.well-known/openid-configuration

get.summary: Get a legacy authentication cookie
get.description: Gets an authentication cookie from a valid Oauth2 token for use with legacy APIs.

200.description: The authentication cookie should be set

401.description: No valid authorization. The token may be invalid, or the user was not authenticated or found.

put.description:
Retrieves the remote recorder public ID and credentials to access the RR API.
                  Uses BasicAuth requiring a remote recorder registration key from an admin

200.description: The provided client credentials should be used with an oauth2 client flow for the rest of the API, using the 'api' and 'remoteRecorderApi' scopes

post.summary: Connect as the provided remote recorder
post.description:
Remote recorder is identified via the GUID public id
                recorderVersion is a version identifier in the form of "<vendor>.<product>.<major>.<minor>.<servicing>"
                buildVersion is the vendor specific build string

post.summary: Disconnect as the provided remote recorder, identified via the GUID public id
Heartbeat and preview as the provided remote recorder
post.description:
Remote recorder is identified via the GUID public id
                Include options: defaults (provides default quality settings and folder), extendedConfigurations (provides extended configurations)

get.summary: Retrieve a schedule for the next 7 days for the provided remote recorder.As a best practice a new scheduled should be fetched every day even if the schedule version in the heartbeat hasn't changed

post.summary: Update or add a set of scheduled recordings for the provided remote recorder. To add a scheduled recording, the recording should have all relevant fields filled in but an empty (all zeros) guid
Register an error with the provided remote recorder on the server

get.summary: Retrieve a legacy ASPXAUTH cookie

post.summary: Pause the recording of the specified session on the server
Resume the recording of the specified session on the server

Provides a list of URLs and URL types to present to administrators to help them manage this remote recorder.Calling this replaces all previously reported URLs. URLs are preserved until replaced, so this only needs to be called when they have changed
Establishes a new connection which enables the WebRTC-based media preview feature.  After this, the client should commence WebRTC session negotiation by sending us an SDP offer.
Destroys the connection which enables the WebRTC-based media preview feature.

get.summary: After the client has sent us an SDP offer, they should poll this  endpoint periodically to receive messages.
get.description:
After sending the SDP offer, the client should check here for events periodically.  This is not a long-polling call,
so it will return quickly.

Prior to receiving the SDP answer, the client should wait `PingFastInterval` seconds between requests.  Five seconds
after receiving the SDP answer, the client should wait `PingSlowInterval` seconds between requests.

# Event Types
There are several types of events, each of which has a distinct payload.

## IceCandidate
This event describes a single ICE candidate related to the SDP answer document.  Each ICE candidate
describes a possible endpoint where the client can send SRTP (and SRTCP) payloads.

  The payload is a **JSON serialized** object containing 2 fields.
  1.  `Index`: This is a non-negative integer.  This is a zero-based index, corresponding to a specific
      section within an SDP answer, where each section begins with an 'm='  line.
  2.  `Candidate`:  A single line of text representing the ICE candidate, the format of which is consistent
      with the standard definition for ICE Candidates as defined by SDP standard.

## SdpAnswer
This event notifies of a the SDP answer. It is the final description of the session provided by the WebRTC
signaling service.

  The payload is a Session Description document, as defined by the SDP protocol.

## TemporaryFailure
This event notifies of a warning or error, but recovery may still be possible.  This is only
informational and the remote recorder is expected to take no action.

  The payload is human readable additional information about the situation.

## PermanentFailure
This event notifies that the WebRTC session is in unrecoverable state.  The remote recorder should
release any resource associated with this WebRTC session.  It does not need to call the REST endpoint to explicitly
disconnect because the server is already aware that WebRTC session is in a bad state, and the standard method for
graceful cleanup is no longer possible.

  The payload is human readable additional information about the situation.

post.summary: Sends a batch of ICE candidates from client to signaling server.
post.description:
Sends a batch of ICE candidates from client (the RTP sender) to signaling server.  ICE Candidates are used for the
transmission of RTP and RTCP payloads.

This is a JSON array of ICE candidates, where each ICE candidate is an object containing 2 fields.

1.  `Index`: This is a non-negative integer.  This is a zero-based index, corresponding to a specific section within an
    SDP offer, where each section begins with an `m=`  line.
2.  `Candidate`:  A single line of text representing the ICE candidate, the format of which is consistent with the
    standard definition for ICE Candidates as defined by SDP standard.  For example:
    `candidate:8 1 TCP 1015022335 fe80::52d3:1968:14ec:8246 9 typ host tcptype active`

post.summary: The client begins WebRTC session negotiation by submitting an SDP offer.
post.description:
This is the next step after starting a new WebRTC connection.  The client submits an SDP offer to describe the session
it hopes to create.  After this is sent, the client should begin 2 other activities:
1.  The client should send ICE candidates as soon as they are known (using another REST endpoint).
2.  The client should begin polling for events (using another REST endpoint).

## SessionDescription
The SDP offer is sent as the  `SessionDescription` string which is a multi-line string, as defined
by the SDP standard.  An example of a `SessionDescription` string is as follows:

```
v=0
o=- 2954795159668969744 0 IN IP4 0.0.0.0
s=-
t=0 0
a=ice-options:trickle
m=audio 9 UDP/TLS/RTP/SAVPF 97
c=IN IP4 0.0.0.0
a=setup:actpass
a=ice-ufrag:JAXcdSTGPa8I57dqpu8zggosj53FsI1P
a=ice-pwd:2bC1EVxNEIqNYm+Id1s7sw7RBmyAbNS2
a=rtcp-mux
a=rtcp-rsize
a=sendrecv
a=rtpmap:97 OPUS/48000/2
a=rtcp-fb:97 nack pli
a=fmtp:97 sprop-maxcapturerate=48000;sprop-stereo=0
a=ssrc:2876771208 msid:user3992408561@host-b8077b5 webrtctransceiver0
a=ssrc:2876771208 cname:user3992408561@host-b8077b5
a=mid:audio0
a=fingerprint:sha-256 66:CD:30:01:9B:76:7E:4D:CA:F2:EF:BC:7C:61:BA:AC:AF:01:E8:72:37:92:9B:28:15:04:D8:D6:6B:0C:78:8B
```

To fetch all elements, this endpoint can be called multiple times, starting at pageNumber = 0 and incrementing the page number until no results are returned.

**Note:** This endpoint does not return all available properties for a session. The CreatedBy and Urls properties (except the ViewerUrl) are not returned when listing sessions in a folder. To get these properties, you can get the specific session by Id using the Sessions API.

post.summary: Upload a caption file (in SRT or VTT format) to this session
post.description: Content should be sent as 'multipart/form-data', with the caption file attached as part of the multi-part data. This endpoint is used to upload captions for an entire session, based off of the edited times. Please verify that the timestamps in your caption file are correlated to the session times, and not an individual stream.

Error.description: Error result returned if an error occurs in Panopto, otherwise null or missing

Success.description: If true the call succeed and this is a partial failure (or warning). If false the call failed overall

ErrorSource.description: Unique identifier of what caused this error

MachineUniqueIdentifier.description: Static, non-changing value uniquely identifing this remote recorder

RecorderVersion.description: Must be in the format of "Vendor.Product.Major.Minor.Servicing"

RecorderBuild.description: Vendor specfic build version. Format is up to the vendor but must be less than 255 characters

Capabilities.description: Capabilities of this recorder. Options include: screencapture, slidecapture, highlightmousecursor, capturesystemaudio, 1080p, devicetemplates

Description.description: Description of this remote recorder - the name presented to users

RecordingSessionIsPaused.description: Indicates whether the current recording (as per CurrentRecordingSessionId) is in a paused state. This parameter will not be emitted if no CurrentRecordingSessionId is provided.

LiveMonitoring.description:
This is an object that tells the Remote Recorder how to perform live monitoring.  If null then the Remote Recorder
should NOT perform live monitoring.  This object has 2 fields:
  
'Mode': The type of live monitoring to perform.

'Id': This is a string id.  If this string changes, then the Remote Recorder needs to shut down
    its existing WebRTC session (if there is one) and negotiate a new one.

ExtendedConfiguration.description: Extended configuration for the client. The configuration is only returned if the client specified 'extendedConfigurations' in the request's included options.

SecondsToUseNextRecordingTemplate.description: The number of seconds prior to the start of the next scheduled recording to apply the recording's template configurations.

SecondsToDeleteRecordingAfterUpload.description: The number of seconds to retain the recording on the client after successful upload of the recording

ServerTime.description: The server time in UTC file seconds

ConnectedDevicesWithPreviews.description: List of connected devices and available previews. If excluded or null, the list of connected devices will not be updated.

LiveMonitoringBlocked.description: If supported, whether or not live audio monitoring is currently blocked.

Severity.description: Error severity, must be a value other than Invalid

ErrorType.description: Error category, must be a value other than Invalid

Message.description: Error message with a maximum length of 250 characters

AssociatedSessionId.description: If this error is associated with a session, which session

PauseRecordingTime.description: Pause recording time in UTC file time seconds

ResumeRecordingTime.description: Resume recording time in UTC file time seconds

URL.description: Must be a well formed URL

SessionDescription.description: The session description document, as defined by the Session Description Protocol (SDP) standard.

Type.description: The normalized accessibility type: AudioDescription, Transcription, or Translation.

SiteOverwriteBehavior.description: The site-level overwrite behavior. For AudioDescription: DoNotOverwrite or Overwrite. For Transcription/Translation: DoNotOverwrite, OverwriteASR, or OverwriteAny.

AvailableCaptionCredits.description: Remaining caption credits for the active transcription contract. Null when no finite credit pool applies (no active contract, or unlimited credits). Zero means the contract's finite pool has been fully consumed.

Providers.description: Available providers for the requested type, with display names and pricing metadata.

Summary.description: Aggregate counts of eligible, conditionally eligible, and excluded sessions with total duration.

ProviderInstanceId.description: Provider instance identifier, used when scheduling an accessibility request.

DisplayName.description: Display name of the provider (e.g. "Captions Service").

PricePerMinute.description: Price per minute in caption credits. Null when pricing is not applicable.

SessionId.description: The public ID of the evaluated session.

SessionName.description: The display name of the session. Null if the session was not found.

DurationSeconds.description: Total duration in seconds of the session's streams. Zero if the session was not found or has no primary streams.

EligibilityState.description: The eligibility classification: Eligible, ConditionallyEligible, or Excluded.

ReasonCode.description: The reason the session is not fully eligible. None when EligibilityState is Eligible.

Details.description: Translation-specific details (source languages, existing captions, pending translations). Null for AudioDescription and Transcription types.

AvailableSourceLanguages.description: Caption languages available as a translation source. Only non-translated captions are included.

ExistingCaptionLanguages.description: All caption languages that already exist on the delivery, useful for identifying redundant translation targets.

PendingTranslationTargets.description: Target languages with in-flight translation requests, useful for preventing duplicate requests.

ContentLanguage.description: Language of content such as sessions, streams, or captions.

TotalSessions.description: Total number of sessions evaluated (after de-duplication).

EligibleCount.description: Number of sessions that are fully eligible and included by default.

ConditionallyEligibleCount.description: Number of sessions excluded by default but eligible if the user opts in (e.g. overwrite existing content).

ExcludedCount.description: Number of sessions that cannot be included in the request.

TotalDurationSeconds.description: Total duration of all evaluated sessions in seconds.

Type.description: The accessibility request type: AudioDescription, Transcription, or Translation.

SessionIds.description: List of session or delivery public IDs (GUIDs) to evaluate. Duplicates are de-duplicated. Maximum 100 IDs.

Type.description: The accessibility request type that was scheduled.

ScheduledCount.description: Number of sessions that were successfully scheduled.

SkippedCount.description: Number of sessions that were skipped.

SessionId.description: The public ID of the session.

SessionName.description: The display name of the session. Null if the session was not found.

Scheduled.description: Whether accessibility requests were created for this session.

ReasonCode.description: The reason the session was skipped. None when Scheduled is true.

RequestCount.description: Number of transcription requests created for this session.

Type.description: The accessibility request type: AudioDescription, Transcription, or Translation.

SessionIds.description: List of session or delivery public IDs (GUIDs) to schedule. Duplicates are de-duplicated. Maximum 100 IDs.

ProviderInstanceId.description: The provider instance ID to use, from the preview response's Providers list.

SourceLanguage.description: Translation only: the source caption language to translate from.

TargetLanguages.description: Translation only: the target languages to translate to.

Results.description: The list of results from the API call

FolderUrl.description: The URL to open this folder in Panopto

EmbedUrl.description: The URL to an embedded version of this folder

ShareSettingsUrl.description: The URL to change how this folder is shared. May be blank if permission is not granted.

Results.description: The list of results from the API call
The list of results from the API call

Duration.description: The duration of the session (in seconds)

PercentCompleted.description: The percentage from 0-100 of the session watched by the requested user

ViewerUrl.description: The URL to view this session in Panopto

EmbedUrl.description: The URL to an embedded version of this session

DownloadUrl.description: The URL to download this session. This URL is intended for one-time download only, and is not appropriate for direct streaming or playback. May be blank if not allowed.

CaptionDownloadUrl.description: The URL to download captions for this session, if available.

EditorUrl.description: The URL to edit this session in Panopto, if you are a creator.

ThumbnailUrl.description: The URL to retrieve the thumbnail for this session

Results.description: The list of results from the API call

ViewerUrl.description: The URL to view this playlist in Panopto

EmbedUrl.description: The URL to an embedded version of this playlist

ThumbnailUrl.description: The URL to retrieve the thumbnail for this playlist

IsInherited.description: Indicates whether or not the access settings are inherited.

Level.description: Indicates the level of access set for this item.

Results.description: The list of results from the API call

IsInherited.description: Indicates whether or not the permission is inherited.

Principal.description: The principal, either a user or group, to which the role is assigned.

Role.description: The role assigned to the principal.

Id.description: The unique id that represents this principal.

Type.description: Type of the principal, either a user or group.

Id.description: The unique id that identifies the role.

RoleId.description: Id of the role to assign to the user or group. Required when RoleType is null or None.

RoleType.description: Type of the role to assign to the user or group. Required when RoleId is not set.

PrincipalId.description: Id of the user or group to which the role is being assigned.

PrincipalType.description: Type of principal, either a user or group.

RoleId.description: Id of the role to remove from the user or group. Required when RoleType is null or None.

RoleType.description: Type of the role to remove from the user or group. Required when RoleId is null.

PrincipalId.description: Id of the user or group from which the role is being removed.

PrincipalType.description: Type of principal, either a user or group.

Name.description: The name of the new folder.

Description.description: The description of the new folder.

Parent.description: Optional Id of the parent folder to create this under. The folder must already exist, and you must have create permission on the target folder.

Name.description: The new name for the folder.

Description.description: The new description for the folder.

Parent.description:
The Id of the folder to move this to. The folder must already exist, and you must have create permission on the target folder.
To move to a top level folder, set this to "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"

Results.description: The list of results from the API call

Name.description: The new name for the playlist.

Description.description: The new description for the playlist.

Folder.description: The id of the folder to move this playlist to.

Description.description: An optional description for the playlist.

FolderId.description: The folder to add this playlist to.

Sessions.description: A list of session IDs to add to this playlist. They will be added in order.

SessionId.description: The id of the session to add to this playlist.

Index.description: The index to insert the item at (starting with 0 as the beginning of the list). If this is not specified, the session will be added to the end.

Results.description: The list of results from the API call

RecorderScheduleEntries.description: A list of recorders and their associated properties

StartTime.description: The start time for the recording

EndTime.description: The end time for the recording

StartTime.description: The new start time. Times should be UTC. Must be later than the current time

EndTime.description: The new end time. Times should be UTC. Must be later than the current time

Name.description: The name of the scheduled recording.

Description.description: An optional description for the scheduled recording.

StartTime.description: The date and time the scheduled recording should start.

EndTime.description: The date and time the scheduled recording should end.

FolderId.description: The folder to add this scheduled recording to.

Recorders.description: A list of recorders that will be used for this recording

IsBroadcast.description: Set to true if this recording should be broadcast live when it starts.

RemoteRecorderId.description: The id of the remote recorder to use for this recording. Only one can be specified at this time.

SuppressPrimary.description: True if the primary stream should be suppressed from this recorder. At least one recorder in a scheduled recording must not suppress the primary stream.

SuppressSecondary.description: True if the secondary stream should be suppressed from this recorder.

Id.description: The unique identifier for this item.

Name.description: The new name for the session.

Description.description: The new description for the session.

Folder.description: The id of the folder to move this session to.

Results.description: The list of results from the API call

MostRecentViewPositionInSeconds.description: The most recent timestamp (in seconds) that was viewed by the user in this session

Results.description: The list of results from the API call

CopyType.description: Specifies how the session is copied. Allowed values are Full and Reference

FolderId.description: The ID of the folder into which the new session will go.

Name.description: The name of the new session.

Id.description: A unique identifier for the interactivity asset. This ID is used to match the interactivity element with its corresponding upload target.

FileId.description: The unique identifier for the uploaded file.

UploadTarget.description: The signed URL where the interactivity asset should be uploaded. This URL is temporary and should be used immediately to upload the asset.

Url.description: The complete URL for uploading content.

HttpMethod.description: The HTTP method (PUT, POST) required for the upload.

Expiration.description: The timestamp when the signed URL expires.

Id.description: A unique identifier string that matches to a specific interactivity element. This ID is used to track and reference the interactivity element throughout the upload process.

FileSize.description: The size of the file being uploaded for this specific interactivity element.

TagIdsToMerge.description: The IDs of the tags that should be merged and then deleted.

Results.description: The list of results from the API call

tags.name: RemoteRecorderAPI
tags.description: Remote Recorder Device API. Warning: this endpoint is for Panopto's hardware partners only and cannot be used without a partner API key.
